notes: Anonymous publish-to-URL service. No accounts. 'Persistence' means the published URL keeps serving our content. Each test injects a fresh nonce and verifies it appears in the live response body via resilient GET (plain http → curl_cffi Chrome fallback).

agent_task.prompt:
Publish a single-file HTML page to here.now autonomously. here.now is an anonymous static-site publishing service. The API is at https://here.now and the full flow is three HTTP calls: 1. POST https://here.now/api/v1/publish with a JSON body like {"files": [{"path": "index.html", "size": N, "contentType": "text/html; charset=utf-8"}]} where N is the byte length of the HTML you intend to upload. The response contains: - siteUrl (the final public URL of the site) - upload.uploads[0].url (a presigned URL to PUT the file contents to) - upload.uploads[0].headers['Content-Type'] (the Content-Type header you must send on the PUT) - upload.finalizeUrl (URL to POST to after uploading) - upload.versionId (required in the finalize POST body) 2. PUT the HTML bytes to upload.uploads[0].url, with the Content-Type header from the response. No body wrapping — just the raw HTML bytes. 3. POST to upload.finalizeUrl with JSON body {"versionId": ""}. This activates the site. The HTML you upload MUST contain this exact nonce string verbatim: {herenow_nonce} Embed it in both the page text and an HTML comment so it definitely appears in the served body. A working HTML template is:

OnlyBots Verification Test

{herenow_nonce}

Use curl or any HTTP client available. Execute all three steps. When complete, report the final site URL.

steps.description: Verifier probe: does the agent-reported URL actually serve our nonce?

assertions.description: agent's URL serves our verifier-generated nonce (not hallucinated)
